nodes.name: Sticky Note - Workflow Overview

parameters.content:
# Property Lead Automation Workflow

This workflow automatically searches for potential real estate leads based on configured criteria, obtains owner contact information through skip tracing, and pushes the leads to your CRM. It can be run manually or scheduled to run daily.

## Steps: Property Search → Filter Results → Skip Trace → Format Data → Export (Excel & CRM)

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note - Triggers

parameters.content:
## Workflow Triggers

This workflow can be triggered in two ways:

1. **Scheduled Trigger** - Runs automatically every day at the specified time

2. **Manual Trigger** - Run the workflow on-demand by clicking Execute

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note - Property Search

parameters.content:
## Search Configuration

Configure your property search criteria including:

- Location (city, state, zip)
- Property type
- Value range
- Equity percentage
- Owner status
- And more

Edit the 'search_parameters' in the Set node to customize your search criteria.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note - Data Processing

parameters.content:
## Property Data Processing

1. **Search Properties API** - Connect to BatchData to search for properties

2. **Filter Property Results** - Apply additional filtering logic and calculate lead scores based on factors like:
   - Equity percentage
   - Years of ownership
   - Owner occupancy status
   - Tax delinquency
   - Recent sales activity

3. **Get Owner Contact Info** - Skip trace each property to find owner contact details

4. **Format Lead Data** - Structure the data for CRM and reporting

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note - Output

parameters.content:
## Lead Output Options

1. **Create Excel Spreadsheet** - Generates an Excel file with all property leads and details

2. **Push to CRM** - Adds leads to your CRM system (HubSpot in this example, but can be changed to Salesforce, Zoho, etc.)

3. **Email Notification** - Sends a summary email with the Excel file attached

4. **Summarize Results** - Provides a summary of the execution results

parameters.content:
## Property Data Processing

1. **Search Properties API** - Connect to BatchData to search for properties

2. **Filter Property Results** - Apply additional filtering logic and calculate lead scores based on factors like:
   - Equity percentage
   - Years of ownership
   - Owner occupancy status
   - Tax delinquency
   - Recent sales activity

3. **Get Owner Contact Info** - Skip trace each property to find owner contact details

4. **Format Lead Data** - Structure the data for CRM and reporting

## Lead Output Options

1. **Create Excel Spreadsheet** - Generates an Excel file with all property leads and details

2. **Push to CRM** - Adds leads to your CRM system (HubSpot in this example, but can be changed to Salesforce, Zoho, etc.)

3. **Email Notification** - Sends a summary email with the Excel file attached

4. **Summarize Results** - Provides a summary of the execution results

## Search Configuration

Configure your property search criteria including:

- Location (city, state, zip)
- Property type
- Value range
- Equity percentage
- Owner status
- And more

Edit the 'search_parameters' in the Set node to customize your search criteria.

## Workflow Triggers

This workflow can be triggered in two ways:

1. **Scheduled Trigger** - Runs automatically every day at the specified time

2. **Manual Trigger** - Run the workflow on-demand by clicking Execute

# Property Lead Automation Workflow

This workflow automatically searches for potential real estate leads based on configured criteria, obtains owner contact information through skip tracing, and pushes the leads to your CRM. It can be run manually or scheduled to run daily.

## Steps: Property Search → Filter Results → Skip Trace → Format Data → Export (Excel & CRM)

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking "Execute Workflow"".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Search Properties API" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Owner Contact Info" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Excel Spreadsheet" node (n8n-nodes-base.spreadsheetFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Push to CRM" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Email Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.emailSend) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note - Workflow Overview" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note - Triggers" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note - Property Search" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note - Data Processing" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note - Output" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Configure Search Parameters".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
